project_type: Go CDK infrastructure project (AWS CDK in Go)
build_system: go mod + cdk synth (cdk CLI installed globally via npm)
test_framework: Go standard testing package with CDK assertions (github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2/assertions)
build_command: cd /projects/sandbox/cdk-sleep-go-kiro && unset NODE_OPTIONS && GOPROXY=direct GONOSUMCHECK=* cdk synth
verification_instructions: 1. Run go test -v ./... and confirm all tests pass. 2. Run cdk synth and confirm it completes without errors.
setup_instructions: 1. Ensure Go 1.25+ is available. 2. Ensure Node.js 22 is available. 3. npm install -g aws-cdk. 4. GOPROXY=direct GONOSUMCHECK=* go mod download. 5. Run go test -v ./... to confirm baseline.
environment_constraints: COMMON_DEPENDENCIES network mode - can install Go modules and npm packages. Must use 'unset NODE_OPTIONS' before running cdk/node commands. Must set GOPROXY=direct GONOSUMCHECK=* for go mod download.
contribution_requirements: Strict TDD: write failing tests first, then minimal code. Keep ARCHITECTURE.md and Mermaid diagram in sync. Conventional commits. Run go test ./... and cdk synth before pushing.
key_patterns: Single-file CDK stack (cdk-base.go) with NewCdkBaseStack function. Single test file (cdk-base_test.go) using assertions.Template_FromStack. L2 constructs used throughout. Package is 'main'. All resources created in NewCdkBaseStack. Tests use jsii.Close() defer pattern.
directory_structure: Flat structure - all Go code at root level. cdk-base.go is the main CDK app. cdk-base_test.go is the test file. ARCHITECTURE.md documents the system.
